task_completion.description: OpenBCI GUI is open and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI application window is present on the screen.

task_completion.description: The GUI is set to Playback mode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Playback' or 'PLAYBACK (from file)' is visible in the GUI interface, indicating the data source.

task_completion.description: The correct EEG recording file is loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filename 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-MotorImagery.txt' is visible within the OpenBCI GUI, typically in the playback control widget or file selection menu.
The playback controls show a 'Play' button (triangle icon) instead of a 'Pause' button (two vertical lines), or there is an explicit 'Paused' status indicator.

task_completion.description: The playback is navigated to the 10-second mark (between 00:08 and 00:12).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The playback timestamp or progress indicator shows a time between 00:08 and 00:12 (or 8.0s to 12.0s).

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenBCI GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI window is active and shows genuine UI elements, not a screenshot or mockup.

integrity.description: The agent didn't just print the expected output in a terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The target file and timestamp are shown in the OpenBCI GUI, not as text output in a terminal window.

integrity.description: The agent didn't type the file name and timestamp into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The information is displayed within the OpenBCI GUI's playback controls, not in a text editor like Gedit or Nano.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file to be loaded is 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-MotorImagery.txt'. The GUI must be in 'Playback' mode. The playback must be paused at a timestamp between 8.0 and 12.0 seconds (00:08 to 00:12).
